reasoning: As of May 16, 2025, there has been no official announcement of a specific date for a presidential election in Ukraine. On the contrary, the Ukrainian parliament (Verkhovna Rada) extended martial law on April 16, 2025, until August 6, 2025, which legally prohibits holding elections. Furthermore, on March 11, 2025, the Rada passed a resolution explicitly affirming that no national elections can take place while the country remains under martial law. While international media (like The Economist) earlier speculated on potential dates such as May 5 for an announcement or May 25 for an election, these were conditional on a ceasefire or the lifting of martial law—neither of which occurred. President Zelenskyy has maintained that elections require 60-90 days of preparation after martial law ends. Given that martial law currently extends past the resolution window's end and the political consensus remains firmly against wartime elections, an announcement before July 1 is highly improbable.
